articles.title: Israeli drone strike kills four Palestinians in Gaza City
articles.summary: Israeli drone strike kills four Palestinians in Gaza City An Israeli drone strike has killed four Palestinians, including a woman, northwest of Gaza City, raising the day’s death toll from Israeli attacks to 13. Medical sources at Al-Shifa Hospital said the Israeli drone fired a missile at a civilian vehicle and passersby near Al-Buraq School in the al-Nasr neighbourhood. Several people were wounded in the attack, some critically, as Israel continued to strike civilians across the besieged enclave. More than 30 people have been wounded in Israeli attacks across Gaza since the start of the day.

articles.title: Israeli strikes kill at least nine in Gaza
articles.summary: Israeli strikes kill at least nine in Gaza Israeli strikes on different locations in Gaza killed at least nine people and wounded 20 others, Palestinian health officials said on Sunday. One strike hit next to a large tent encampment of displaced families in Khan Younis in the south of the enclave, killing five and wounding 16 others. Later on Sunday, a separate Israeli air strike killed four people and wounded four others when it hit a vehicle driving through the middle of Gaza City. articles.title: Trump calls for more 'surgical' strikes on Hezbollah
articles.summary: Trump calls for more 'surgical' strikes on Hezbollah US President Donald Trump has called for more "surgical" attacks on Hezbollah in Lebanon, while at the same time saying he was "very close" to a deal with Iran. The comments, made to NBC's Meet the Press programme in an interview recorded on Friday but broadcast on Sunday, have been interpreted as a tacit blessing of Israeli strikes on Beirut's southern suburbs. “I would like to see Lebanon experience a better life. I would like to see a more surgical attack against Hezbollah. I think it should be more surgical,” Trump said.

articles.summary: Israel bombs Beirut, with multiple blasts heard Israel bombed Lebanon's capital, Beirut, again on Sunday, with at least three explosions heard so far.  According to witnesses on the ground, a multi-storey building has been hit. The Israeli military said it had struck "terrorist" headquarters in Beirut's southern suburbs, in the Dahieh area. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Defence Minister Israel Katz said in a joint statement that Israel was responding to Hezbollah firing towards Israeli territory.

articles.title: Opec+ set to increase oil output targets again
articles.summary: Opec+ set to increase oil output targets again Opec+ is set to agree on Sunday on a fourth increase in oil output targets in as many months, three sources from the oil cartel told Reuters, even though the war on Iran continues to prevent several members from increasing production. The war has disrupted oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz, creating the world's biggest supply crisis as key Opec+ members, including Saudi Arabia, have been unable to supply customers in full since the end of February, Reuters reported.
